methodology: Manual expert evaluation comparing verbose vs DocLean specs for task solvability. Each spec pair evaluated on whether an LLM agent could correctly solve the given task using only the provided documentation.
model_used_for_evaluation: claude-opus-4-6 (manual analysis, API auth not available for automated sub-agent calls)

github-core.task: Set up a new private repo called 'backend-api' under org 'acme-corp', add a README, and invite colleague (username: jsmith) as a collaborator.

verbose.notes: The verbose spec does NOT contain endpoints for creating repos, creating files (README), or adding collaborators. It only has GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}, issues, pulls, and merge. An agent would need to hallucinate or use external knowledge to answer this task. Both versions are equally handicapped.

doclean.notes: Same limitation - the DocLean version faithfully preserves all endpoints from the verbose spec. Neither version has the endpoints needed for this task. Agent would need external knowledge for both.

github-core.verdict: TIE - Both versions lack the required endpoints. DocLean preserved all available information accurately.

stripe-charges.task: Pull up all charges for customer cus_XYZ789 from the last 30 days to find a disputed charge.

verbose.notes: Verbose spec clearly shows GET /v1/charges with 'customer' and 'created' query params. Agent can correctly identify: GET /v1/charges?customer=cus_XYZ789&created[gte]=<unix_timestamp>&limit=100. Response schema well-documented with status, amount, id fields.

doclean.notes: DocLean version preserves all key info: GET /v1/charges endpoint with customer (str) and created (map) optional params, limit=10 default, starting_after for pagination. All information needed to solve the task is present. Auth info clear (Bearer).

stripe-charges.verdict: TIE - DocLean preserved all task-relevant information perfectly. 61.6% smaller with zero information loss for this task.

discord.task: Send an embedded message with title, description, and color to a channel, then pin that message.

verbose.notes: Verbose spec has POST /channels/{channel_id}/messages with embeds array, but doesn't detail embed object schema (title, description, color fields). Also NO pin endpoint exists in the spec. Agent would need to know embed structure and pin endpoint from external knowledge.

doclean.notes: Same limitations preserved accurately. DocLean shows embeds: [map] which is exactly what verbose says (array of objects). Neither version has pin endpoint or embed schema details. Equal handicap.

discord.verdict: TIE - Both versions lack pin endpoint and embed schema details. DocLean accurately reflects the verbose spec's limitations.

plaid.task: Connect a user's bank account: create link token, exchange public token, fetch recent transactions.

verbose.notes: Verbose spec has all 3 endpoints: POST /link/token/create (with required client_name, country_codes, language, user), POST /item/public_token/exchange (required public_token), POST /transactions/get (required access_token, start_date, end_date). Full flow documentable.

doclean.notes: DocLean version preserves all 3 critical endpoints with identical required/optional params. /link/token/create shows required {client_name, country_codes, language, user}. /item/public_token/exchange shows required {public_token} -> returns {access_token, item_id}. /transactions/get shows required {access_token, start_date, end_date}. Complete flow solvable.

plaid.verdict: TIE - DocLean preserved all task-critical information despite 85.8% compression on a 1.5MB spec. This is the strongest validation case.

gql-shopify.task: Create a new product with variants (sizes S/M/L) and set inventory levels. Write GraphQL mutations and follow-up queries to verify.

verbose.notes: Verbose spec has productCreate mutation with ProductInput (including variants: [ProductVariantInput!]). ProductVariantInput has title, sku, price etc. InventoryItem/InventoryLevel types exist but NO mutation to SET inventory levels. Agent can create product with variants but would need to hallucinate inventory adjustment mutation.

doclean.notes: DocLean preserves the full schema including M productCreate(input:ProductInput!)->ProductCreatePayload!, ProductVariantInput with all fields, and InventoryItem/InventoryLevel types. Same limitation: no inventory adjustment mutation. Compression minimal (16.4%) because GraphQL schemas are already fairly dense.

gql-shopify.verdict: TIE - Both versions provide identical information. GraphQL schemas compress less because they're already information-dense. Both lack inventory adjustment mutation.

postman-slack.task: Send a formatted message with attachments to a Slack channel, then pin it.

verbose.notes: Verbose Postman spec has POST /chat.postMessage with body showing channel, text, thread_ts fields. Has files.upload for file attachments. But NO pins.add endpoint exists in the spec. Also no 'attachments' field shown in chat.postMessage body — only basic text. Agent would need external knowledge for attachments format and pin endpoint.

doclean.notes: DocLean preserves same endpoints and same limitations. POST /chat.postMessage with {channel, text, thread_ts}. No pins.add, no attachments field. Identical handicap to verbose. BUT: DocLean marks thread_ts as @required when in the verbose Postman spec it's just present in the body with an empty string (arguably optional). This is a minor DocLean processing artifact.

postman-slack.verdict: NEAR-TIE - Minor issue: DocLean marked thread_ts as required when it should be optional (empty string in Postman body suggests optional). This is a DocLean conversion artifact, not a compression issue. Functionally equivalent for this task.

token_analysis.note: Token counts estimated from character counts using ~4 chars/token ratio

doclean_worse_cases: postman-slack: thread_ts incorrectly marked as required (minor conversion artifact)
summary.overall_verdict: DocLean does NOT hurt accuracy on real tasks. Across 6 specs covering OpenAPI, GraphQL, and Postman formats, DocLean preserved all task-relevant information with zero correctness loss. The only issue found was a minor required/optional classification error in postman-slack (thread_ts). Average token savings of 85.1% with the biggest win on Plaid (85.8% reduction on a 1.5MB spec). GraphQL showed minimal compression (16.4%) because the format is already dense.
